name: Bundles/batch
description: Bundle type=batch conformance tests, ported from the Microsoft FHIR Server E2E suite (BundleBatchTests.cs). Unlike a transaction, a batch Bundle processes each entry independently: one entry's failure must not abort or roll back the others. POSTs a type=batch Bundle to the FHIR base URL (same POST-to-base-URL mechanism as Bundles/transaction.json — the engine has no dedicated batch operation code, so type.code='batch' is unrecognised by DeriveMethod and falls through to POST, and with no url/resource/params the request URL resolves to the server base). Bundle bodies are static fixtures using either server-assigned ids (plain POST) or a fixed client-supplied id (PUT), per the engine's static-fixture-body constraint.

test.name: Batch processes every entry independently; a failing entry does not abort the others
test.description: POST a batch Bundle of 4 entries to the base URL: (1) a Patient create, (2) a GET of a deliberately nonexistent Patient (expected to fail with 404), (3) another Patient create, (4) a Patient create-via-PUT with a client-supplied id. Unlike a transaction, the batch must return 200 with all 4 per-entry results present — the 404 in entry 2 must not prevent entries 3 and 4 from succeeding (no all-or-nothing rollback).

operation.description: POST batch Bundle (create, failing GET, create, PUT) to the base URL

assert.description: Batch submission itself must return HTTP 200 (per-entry failures do not fail the outer batch)
Response must be a Bundle
Response Bundle must contain exactly 4 entries (one per submitted request)
Entry 1 (Patient create) must succeed with a 2xx status
Entry 2 (GET of a nonexistent Patient) must independently fail with 404
Entry 3 (Patient create) must still succeed — entry 2's failure must not abort subsequent entries
Entry 4 (Patient create-via-PUT) must still succeed

test.name: Conditional create (If-None-Exist) inside a batch is idempotent across repeated submissions
test.description: POST a batch Bundle containing a single conditional-create Patient entry twice. The first submission must create the resource (201); resubmitting the identical batch must match the existing resource rather than create a duplicate (200), mirroring the Microsoft FHIR Server's GivenAValidBundle_WhenSubmittingABatchTwiceWithAndWithoutChanges_ThenVersionIsCreatedWhenDataIsChanged scenario.

operation.description: POST batch Bundle with conditional-create Patient (1st submission)

assert.description: First submission must return HTTP 200
First submission's entry must report 201 Created

operation.description: POST the identical batch Bundle again (2nd submission)

assert.description: Second submission must also return HTTP 200
Second submission's entry must report 200 (matched existing, not re-created)

test.name: No rollback: resources from before and after the failing entry both persist
test.description: After the batch in the previous test ran (including its internal 404 failure), independently verify that BATCH-1 (before the failure), BATCH-2 (after the failure), and the client-id BATCH-3 Patient are all persisted, and that the conditional-create BATCH-4 Patient exists exactly once despite being submitted twice.

assert.description: BATCH-1 search must return HTTP 200
BATCH-1 (created before the failing entry) must be persisted
BATCH-2 search must return HTTP 200
BATCH-2 (created after the failing entry) must also be persisted — proves no rollback
BATCH-3 (created via PUT with client id) must be readable
Read must return a Patient
BATCH-4 search must return HTTP 200
BATCH-4 must exist exactly once — the conditional create must not have created a duplicate on resubmission

test.name: Posting a non-Bundle resource to the base URL is rejected
test.description: POST a plain Observation (not wrapped in a Bundle) to the base URL, mirroring GivenANonBundleResource_WhenSubmittingABatch_ThenBadRequestIsReturned. The base URL only accepts batch/transaction Bundles, so the server is expected to reject this with 400. Marked warningOnly because non-Bundle-at-root handling is not explicitly mandated by the FHIR spec and some servers may respond with a different error status.

operation.description: POST a plain Observation (not a Bundle) to the base URL

assert.description: Server should reject a non-Bundle root submission with 400 Bad Request

test.name: A Bundle missing the required type element is rejected
test.description: POST a Bundle with no 'type' element to the base URL. The Microsoft FHIR Server returns 405 Method Not Allowed ('Bundle type is not present. Possible values are: transaction or batch'); other conformant servers may instead reject this at structural validation with 400. Both are marked warningOnly since the exact status code for this malformed submission is not spec-mandated.

operation.description: POST a Bundle with no type element to the base URL

assert.description: Alternative: 405 Method Not Allowed (Microsoft FHIR Server behavior)
Alternative: 400 Bad Request (structural validation rejection)

operation.description: Teardown: defensive conditional delete in case a lenient server processed the typeless Bundle
Teardown: defensive conditional delete in case a lenient server created the non-Bundle Observation
